title: gnaphalium rigidulum
intro: Gnaphalium rigidulum, also known as stiff cudweed, is a perennial plant species in the family Asteraceae, characterized by its hairy stems and small white flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits associated with stiff cudweed include relief from arthritis and other inflammatory conditions, as well as the potential to reduce fever and soothe skin irritations. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and it is also used in some herbal remedies for its purported health benefits.
